output: Batch 4 complete (T7, T9). **T7**: Rewrote `useClipResize.ts` with three new helpers (`resolveGroupMembers`, `getInteriorNeighbor`, `computeClipResizeMetaPatch`). `startGroupEdgeResize` now resolves first/last clip via `orderClipIdsByAt` and emits `{type:'rows'}` mutating only that clip. `onActionResizeEnd` detects interior-boundary via `getInteriorNeighbor` and emits a two-clip rows mutation (primary + symmetric neighbor) so the neighbor's far edge stays fixed. Outer edges and free clips share the single-clip path. New `useClipResize.test.tsx` with 7 cases passes (first-edge, last-edge, interior-boundary right, interior-boundary left symmetric, resize\u2192resize sequence with updated dataRef, startGroupEdgeResize right, startGroupEdgeResize left). **T9**: Dropped `PlannedGroupMove` + the groupMoves branch from `multi-drag-utils.ts`. `planMultiDragMoves` now expands grouped drag into per-clip `PlannedMove` entries. `applyMultiDragMoves` return type no longer includes `nextPinnedShotGroups`/`nextConfig`. Added a `rebuildGroupAfterDrag` helper in `useClipDrag.ts` that updates `trackId` + re-derives `clipIds` via `orderClipIdsByAt` from post-commit rows. Same-track grouped drags now emit `{type:'rows'}` + soft-tag override; new-track grouped drags emit a single `{type:'config'}` + soft-tag override. Removed reads of `.children`/`.start` from `enclosingGroup` (now computed from live member-clip bounds). `multi-drag-utils.test.ts` rewritten (10 tests including 3 new planMultiDragMoves group cases + rewritten applyMultiDragMoves clip-path test). New `useClipDrag.softtag.test.tsx` with 5 cases covers no-collision, colliding, cross-track existing-row, new-track, and identity preservation of non-dragged groups. **62 tests across 12 files pass** including resolve-overlaps regression checks. Zero new tsc errors; only 2 pre-existing baseline errors remain. The legacy `useClipDrag.test.tsx` is left alone (still imports T5-deleted helpers; T15 will clean it up alongside the broader test surface).
deviations: Legacy src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx still imports buildGroupFromChildren and projectPinnedShotGroups (both deleted in T5) and uses makePinnedGroup({start, children, ...}) at lines 11 and 170-195. Rather than field-strip this file in the middle of T9, I created a new sibling file useClipDrag.softtag.test.tsx with 5 focused regression tests for the new soft-tag drag behavior. The legacy file remains uncollectable by vitest (import error), but it's explicitly scheduled for cleanup in T15. This keeps T9 scoped to the implementation + targeted regression coverage as the plan requires. If reviewer prefers the legacy file to be field-stripped now, flag it and I can follow up.

Kept findBestGroupStart / GroupExtent in resolve-overlaps.ts because they are still used by applyMultiDragMoves' internal clip-path overlap resolver for same-track group overlap clamping (verified resolve-overlaps.test.ts still passes). The plan said 'remove if unused' — since they ARE still used, kept them. Net LOC in resolve-overlaps.ts unchanged; net LOC in multi-drag-utils.ts and useClipDrag.ts DECREASED.

task_updates.executor_notes: Rewrote src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts. Dropped imports of resizeChildBoundary/resizeGroupEdge (deleted in T5). New internal helpers: resolveGroupMembers (ordered clipIds + actionsById from live rows via orderClipIdsByAt), getInteriorNeighbor (detects interior-boundary via orderClipIdsByAt + adjacency), computeClipResizeMetaPatch (pure per-clip patch: hold for hold clips, speed-based from/to math for visual media, from/to math for audio), applyActionBounds (pure rows update). onActionResizeEnd branches into (A) interior-boundary via getInteriorNeighbor: computes primary patch + symmetric neighbor patch (dir='left' when primary dir='right' and vice versa), builds two-clip rows mutation via applyActionBounds x2 + merged metaUpdates, commits as {type:'rows'}; (B) outer-edge/free clip: single-clip rows mutation with resolveOverlaps + computeClipResizeMetaPatch. startGroupEdgeResize resolves first/last clip from orderClipIdsByAt(group.clipIds,{rows})[0 or len-1], captures origin once, returns a driver that emits {type:'rows'} mutating only that clip's at/hold/source-time. New test file src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx (7 cases, all pass): first-edge only-first-clip, last-edge only-last-clip, interior dir=right two-clip trade with c's right edge fixed, interior dir=left symmetric case, sequence resize\u2192resize with updated dataRef, startGroupEdgeResize right mutates last only, startGroupEdgeResize left mutates first only. tsc on useClipResize.ts: zero errors.

Rewrote grouped drag end-to-end. (1) src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts: Removed PlannedGroupMove type and the entire groupMoves branch from applyMultiDragMoves (along with its now-dead moveGroup call). planMultiDragMoves now expands a grouped drag into per-clip PlannedMove entries: iterates group.clipIds, resolves each member's initial start from clipOffsets (or live rows fallback), emits one clip move per member translated by timeDelta to anchorTargetRowId. Track-kind compatibility check retained. Removed moveGroup and buildDataFromCurrentRegistry imports. applyMultiDragMoves return type no longer includes nextPinnedShotGroups/nextConfig. findBestGroupStart/GroupExtent retained in resolve-overlaps.ts because still used by the internal clip-path overlap resolver. (2) multi-drag-utils.test.ts: Deleted 'routes group entities through moveGroup' test. Added 3 new planMultiDragMoves cases (same-track grouped expansion, cross-track grouped expansion, incompatible-kind rejection) and a rewritten applyMultiDragMoves clip-path test asserting no nextPinnedShotGroups/nextConfig on the return. 10 tests pass. (3) src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts: Removed reads of enclosingGroup.group.children and .start. groupLiveStart/groupLiveEnd now computed from the member clips' live actions in current.rows. initialStart, clipDuration, and groupDragEntry.originStart all derive from these live bounds. Added local rebuildGroupAfterDrag helper that passes non-dragged groups through unchanged and, for the dragged group, updates trackId to newTrackId and re-derives clipIds via orderClipIdsByAt({rows: nextRows}). Drag commit rewritten: same-track / existing-row cross-track path emits single {type:'rows', rows, metaUpdates, clipOrderOverride, pinnedShotGroupsOverride: rebuildGroupAfterDrag(...)}; new-track path emits single {type:'config', resolvedConfig: finalConfig, pinnedShotGroupsOverride: rebuildGroupAfterDrag(...newTrackId, nextRows)}. Soft-tag override is ALWAYS included when session.groupDragEntry is present \u2014 no hard-coded 'no override needed'. The prior applyEdit({type:'pinnedShotGroups'}) branch is gone. pinnedShotGroupsOverride typing is implicitly tightened to soft-tag by T1 (PinnedShotGroup type no longer has start/children). New test file src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.softtag.test.tsx (5 cases, all pass): same-track no-collision, same-track colliding with free clip, cross-track existing-row with trackId update, cross-track new-track via buildAugmentedData, rebuildGroupAfterDrag identity preservation for non-dragged groups. Verified across the batch surface: 62 tests across 12 files pass (including resolve-overlaps regression). tsc targeted on useClipDrag.ts, multi-drag-utils.ts, useTimelineCommit.ts: zero new errors; only 2 pre-existing baseline errors remain (useClipDrag.ts:481 boolean|undefined and multi-drag-utils.ts:130 flatMap typing \u2014 confirmed both in baseline via git stash).

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. First-edge, last-edge, and interior-boundary resize operations all emit applyEdit({type:'rows'}) touching only the semantically correct clip(s). First-edge and last-edge take the single-clip path (only the first or last clip of the group mutates; group cohesion is implicit because only that clip changed). Interior-boundary takes a two-clip path: primary clip's hold changes via computeClipResizeMetaPatch with the dragged dir; neighbor is updated via the symmetric computeClipResizeMetaPatch (dir flipped) so its far edge stays fixed. Both clips merged into a single rows mutation via buildRowTrackPatches + both per-clip meta patches. startGroupEdgeResize resolves first/last from orderClipIdsByAt(group.clipIds,{rows})[0 or len-1] and emits a rows mutation on just that clip. The sequence test (last-edge resize \u2192 interior-boundary) passes \u2014 after step 1 updates dataRef via a simulated materializeData, step 2 reads the updated live `at` of c (end=3.5) and correctly shrinks c to hold=1.3 when the b/c boundary moves to 2.2, keeping c's right edge at 3.5. All 7 test cases pass.

Confirmed. Same-track grouped drags emit {type:'rows'} with a soft-tag pinnedShotGroupsOverride whenever session.groupDragEntry is present \u2014 no hard-coded 'no override needed'. The override is built by rebuildGroupAfterDrag which re-derives clipIds via orderClipIdsByAt({rows: nextRows}) from the post-applyMultiDragMoves (post-resolveOverlaps) rows. New-track grouped drags emit a single {type:'config'} commit carrying the new track (via buildAugmentedData \u2192 buildConfigFromDragResult) plus a soft-tag override whose trackId = newTrackId and clipIds = orderClipIdsByAt. No second applyEdit call, no applyEdit({type:'pinnedShotGroups'}) branch remains in the drag commit path. pinnedShotGroupsOverride typing is implicitly tightened to the soft-tag shape on BOTH variants because TimelineData['config']['pinnedShotGroups'] resolves to PinnedShotGroup[] after T1 removed start/children \u2014 geometry cannot leak back in at the type level. The softtag test file proves: same-track no-collision, same-track colliding with free clip, cross-track existing-row trackId update, cross-track new-track trackId update, and non-dragged group identity preservation. All 5 cases pass. Incompatible-kind grouped drag is rejected by planMultiDragMoves (verified in multi-drag-utils.test.ts). The legacy useClipDrag.test.tsx is left alone (imports T5-deleted helpers; T15 will clean it up).
